kenka stance; stiff arm them to shift their balance off closer foot; step in to hook their foot from behind it; bring arm close so youre touching; pull them down to fall. Variation: similar to hiza guruma — place back foot behind front foot; hook uke's inner foot and pull down.
similar to o uchi gari; dominant foot sweeps uke's opposite foot toward you with a half step de ashi rhythm; point other foot in direction of throw; blanket shake arms to bring uke up then down
use arm to lift uke; sweep their feet into each other.
kenka yotsu stance; step betwen uke's legs; turn and pull uke forward onto hip; turn uke while sweeping inner leg from the inside; flip and roll over.
tori rotates back to uke offset at the hip 70/30; far ankle blocks uke's far ankle; tori lowers as pulling uke over leg; tori raises to pop uke over leg.
ai yotsu stance gripping upper lapel; step in front of uke's outer foot; turn while pulling uke forward onto hip; turn uke while sweeping outer leg from the outside; flip and roll over.

Like o goshi but wrap deep around uke's neck and drive toward the mat; variation: stick out hip/leg and fall toward uke's foot ("ass koshi guruma"); defense: block with arm or pick tori up; counter: transition into seoi nage.
ai yotsu stance; tori turns 180 pulling uke's sleeve over like a seatbelt; lapel elbow bends into their armpit; tori squats and pulls uke over lower back like uki goshi.
tori turns opposite direction; pulling sleeve across face to the same side as other arm; uke's thumb down and armpit tight against tori; tori pulls uke into a flip.
